title: Should I buy the series on VHS for 120
display_title: Should I buy the series on VHS for 120
wikitext:
[]: Should I buy the series on VHS for $120?             Quote Post by Aoren » Sun Oct 12, 2014 10:03 am [Topic title expanded for clarification. - Monk] I always wanted to be an evangelion collector, the thing is I'm a poor fag. Yesterday I found a website with a whole complete collection of the series in VHS for 120 dollars on apparent excellent condition (except director's cut). Do you guys think it's a good idea to start my collection with that? Whenever I pass these things I regret it when I don't see them in a future research, every year is a year lost as these things become more difficult to find. I think I have a problem.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Oct 12, 2014 12:48 pm since i prefer the non-director's cut version anyway, yes, buy that. Edit - but yeah, as others have pointed out that price is crazy.

[]:             Quote Post by Baz » Sun Oct 12, 2014 1:03 pm Check the shipping cost first.

[]:             Quote Post by ThanatosII » Sun Oct 12, 2014 1:13 pm As cool as getting a VHS set sounds, that's a $120 box that will collect dust... So long as you pace yourself in your collection dreams, it can't be that bad. Just don't go crazy after that purchase thinking you need to get everything Eva :p

[]: Re: Should I?             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sun Oct 12, 2014 1:24 pm Aoren wrote: I always wanted to be an evangelion collector, the thing is I'm a poor fag. Yesterday I found a website with a whole complete collection of the series in VHS for 120 dollars on apparent excellent condition (except director's cut). Do you guys think it's a good idea to start my collection with that?

[]:             Quote Post by TitaniumViper » Sun Oct 12, 2014 1:59 pm $120 dollars is a bit much for VHS tapes. I got all mine (Although used, but in good condition) for $15.

[]:             Quote Post by Compiling_Autumn » Sun Oct 12, 2014 2:01 pm I got the Perfect Collection and the End of Evangelion DVD for $70 on eBay. Then I picked up the 21-23 DC DVD on eBay for $8. So, no, spending $120 on a VHS set is not a good way to start your collection. If I were you, I'd get on eBay and try and get it all, either at once or in pieces. It'll be much cheaper and better that way

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Oct 12, 2014 8:44 pm The only way an Evangelion collection on VHS would be worth anything around $100 would be if every tape was factory sealed mint. If it's a complete set, and it's in excellent shape, I would offer no higher than $50. And even that is pretty generous. VHS tapes are worth diddly dick. They're almost as worthless as cassette tapes.

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Sun Oct 12, 2014 9:06 pm Do you have a VHS to DVD/MP3 converter? If they haven't been taken care of then they could fall apart in the VCR.

[]:             Quote Post by Aoren » Mon Oct 13, 2014 12:42 pm Hello! Thanks for all your replies. This is how they look like: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Oct 13, 2014 12:54 pm I’ve found Eva VHS Tapes at second-hand thrift stores for 99¢ a piece. Any pre-opened VHS tape probably isn’t worth too much more than that. $5 per tape tops is what I’d spend on opened dated delicate technological materials.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Tue Oct 14, 2014 3:46 pm To give you some more perspective - Surugaya-ya has used laserdiscs and VHS starting at 55¥ each . Surprisingly or not, several LDS are actually the lowest. I see from the pic that these are "English Language Version". Originally those were $25 each vs. $30 each for the dub. Like everyone else, my thoughts on this is that $120 even for a complete set is too high. My experience with the VHS is that people are usually trying to unload them, so they normally will sell them real cheap. Since you've expressed a preference for the series on VHS I will respect your decision. Aoren wrote: Right now I have barely any Eva merchandise, when I said I wanted to be a collector in a future I meant to have as possible much Eva as exists. You know, decorate a cabinet with lots of Eva stuff - like Helga Pataki's closet lmao.

[]:             Quote Post by Aoren » Wed Oct 15, 2014 5:08 pm Ok guys. I bought it. I spoke with the seller and used all the words you gave me to haggle. I said it's obsolete technology, that it's the value of the collection on DVD, and so on. I asked several things that I knew he was going to reply no. Like, if he included the director's cut and stuff. Then I told him I could not pay that much for them if they did not have all those things. I ended up buying them for 70 dollars, free shipping. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Thu Oct 16, 2014 1:01 pm Aoren wrote: I still don't know if was worth it. I'm happy and all but I don't know anything about VHS.

[]:             Quote Post by Aoren » Thu Oct 16, 2014 1:53 pm That was really helpful. Thanks for enlighten me! I will be alert to the updates of these collections. I'm already building a cabinet for these VHS. I'm really excited.

[]:             Quote Post by ::KL7:: » Thu Oct 16, 2014 2:27 pm That's Awesome. Congratulations, a starts a start. And even better that you were able to get them for less than the original offer. :).

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Oct 16, 2014 3:12 pm Glad you haggled the price down. If you want the DVD versions there are some cheap options on Amazon: http://www.amazon.com/Neon-Genesis-Evangelion-Perfect-Collection/dp/B0000639E0/ref=sr_1_6?s=movies-tv&ie=UTF8&qid=1413489942&sr=1-6&keywords=evangelion Not the platinums or thinpacks, but still the complete collection. ebay likewise has some really good deals, including the platinum set for $20: http://www.ebay.com/bhp/evangelion-dvd

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Oct 16, 2014 6:24 pm Aoren wrote: That was really helpful. Thanks for enlighten me! I will be alert to the updates of these collections. I'm already building a cabinet for these VHS. I'm really excited.

[]:             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Thu Oct 16, 2014 9:01 pm Are these official tapes or taped-off-TV? If it's the latter, then are these from Japan or America?

[]:             Quote Post by Aoren » Thu Oct 16, 2014 10:22 pm ::KL7:: wrote: That's Awesome. Congratulations, a starts a start. And even better that you were able to get them for less than the original offer. :).
